kp6:1.4: & listen intently to what I say.
kp6:2.1: Thus, spirits, you should all be attentive.
kp6:2.2: Show goodwill to the human race.

kp6:4.3: There is nothing to equal that Dhamma.

kp6:5.1: What the excellent Awakened One extolled as pure
kp6:5.2: and called the concentration of unmediated knowing:
kp6:5.3: No equal to that concentration can be found.
kp6:5.4: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Dhamma.
kp6:5.5: By this truth may there be well-being.
kp6:6.1: The eight persons—the four pairs— praised by those at peace:
kp6:6.3: They, disciples of the One Well-Gone, deserve offerings.
kp6:6.4: What is given to them bears great fruit.
kp6:6.5: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Saṅgha.
kp6:6.6: By this truth may there be well-being.

kp6:8.1: An Indra pillar, planted in the earth,
kp6:8.2: that even the four winds cannot shake:
kp6:8.3: That, I tell you, is like the person of integrity,
kp6:8.5: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Saṅgha.
kp6:8.6: By this truth may there be well-being.
kp6:9.1: Those who have seen clearly the noble truths
kp6:9.2: well-taught by the one deeply discerning—
kp6:9.3: regardless of what [later] might make them heedless—
kp6:9.4: will come to no eighth state of becoming.
kp6:9.5: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Saṅgha.
kp6:9.6: By this truth may there be well-being.

kp6:11.2: and incapable of committing the six great wrongs.
kp6:11.3: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Saṅgha.
kp6:11.4: By this truth may there be well-being.

kp6:12.5: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Saṅgha.

kp6:13.1: Like a forest grove with flowering tops
kp6:13.2: in the first month of the heat of the summer,
kp6:13.3: so is the foremost Dhamma he taught,
kp6:13.4: for the highest benefit, leading to unbinding.
kp6:13.5: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Buddha.
kp6:13.6: By this truth may there be well-being.

kp6:14.3: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Buddha.
kp6:14.4: By this truth may there be well-being.
kp6:15.1: Ended the old, there is no new taking birth.
kp6:15.2: Dispassioned their minds toward future becoming,
kp6:15.3: they, with no seed, no desire for growth,
kp6:15.4: the enlightened, go out like this flame.
kp6:15.5: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Saṅgha.
kp6:15.6: By this truth may there be well-being.
